mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions, which boosts consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving S&P 500 forward returns higher over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE declined from 10.0% to 9.6% over the past 3 months
failure_condition: When UNRATE drops, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative more than 40% of the time, or rank correlation with UNRATE changes is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation above 2% annualized triggers market expectations of Federal Reserve tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:In 2010, Fed maintained near-zero rates but inflation expectations were monitored closely
failure_condition: In months following CPI increases above 0.3%, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive more than 50% of the time, or the mean return is not statistically lower than in low-inflation months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and housing starts signal broad-based economic expansion, driving corporate profits and capital investment up, with a lag, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST rose from 590 to 610 in recent months, INDPRO stabilized
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and HOUST rise month-over-month, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative more than 50% of the time, or the joint signal's Sharpe ratio is below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth is a particularly powerful driver of equity returns when the economy is still operating below potential GDP, as it directly reflects consumer spending recovery that lifts earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13260.7 remains below pre-recession peak of ~14600
regime_hint: economy below potential output during recovery
failure_condition: When GDPC1 is below its prior peak and RSAFS grows month-over-month, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are not significantly higher than in periods without such conditions (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the unemployment rate below a key threshold (9.0%) breaks the perception of a weak labor market and triggers rapid repricing of equities upward as investors anticipate sustained expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE has declined from 10.0% to 9.6% in recent months
regime_hint: high unemployment period with clear downward trend
failure_condition: When UNRATE drops below 9.0%, forward 5-day S&P 500 returns are negative more than half the time, or the average return in the 5 days after trigger is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation erodes real earnings and signals future monetary tightening, which puts downward pressure on equity valuations regardless of the prevailing regime.
evidence_refs: fact:CPI index stands at 218.372, up from 215.969 one year ago
failure_condition: The rank correlation between monthly CPI changes and subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns is consistently positive and significant over any 2-year rolling window in the walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent positive month-over-month growth in both nonfarm payrolls and industrial production confirms broad economic momentum, leading to increased corporate profitability and positive S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and INDPRO have positive month-over-month growth
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS increased by 151,000 in October 2010, INDPRO was flat
failure_condition: When both PAYEMS and INDPRO show positive MoM growth, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are negative more than 40% of the time, or the average return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: At the zero lower bound for the federal funds rate, retail sales growth becomes an amplified positive signal for equities because monetary policy cannot ease further, making any demand improvement especially valuable.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is 0.19%, effectively at zero lower bound
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and RSAFS grows month-over-month, forward 20-day S&P 500 returns are not significantly higher than in similar periods with FEDFUNDS above 0.25% (two-sample t-test p > 0.10).
